subject: Mathematics
title: Pascal: Prodigy and Philosopher
description: A micro-lesson that makes Pascal: Prodigy and Philosopher usable.

hook.text: At age 16, Blaise Pascal wrote a groundbreaking paper on conic sections. Descartes refused to believe it.

buildup.text: He invented one of the first mechanical calculators to help his tax-collector father.

discovery.text: His triangle of binomial coefficients unlocked counting, probability, and algebra all at once.

twist.text: At 31, a religious experience led him to abandon math for theology. He never returned.

climax.text: In his short career, he laid foundations for probability, fluid mechanics, and computing.

punchline.text: A teenage genius who walked away from math at 31.

quiz.question: What did Pascal invent to help his father?
